summary: The CI job failed because two test assertions in test_tcl failed. The tests expect that surrogate pairs (\ud83d\udcbb) are decoded into the corresponding Unicode character (U+1F4BB, 💻), but Tcl 9.0.3 is returning the UTF-8 byte sequence interpreted as latin-1 characters ('ðŸ'»') instead.
root_cause: test_eval_surrogates_in_result and test_evalfile_surrogates_in_result in test.test_tcl fail because tcl.eval returns '<\ud83d\udcbb>' (surrogate pair not merged) instead of '<\U0001f4bb>' (the expected merged codepoint). The Tcl 9.0.3 library bundled in the externals directory handles the surrogate pair differently than the test expects, resulting in an encoding mismatch.

evidence.reason: This is the direct assertion failure: the actual value returned by Tcl contains the surrogate pair unmerged, while the expected value is the correctly merged emoji codepoint.
The test expects surrogate pairs in Tcl input to be decoded into the combined codepoint U+1F4bb, but Tcl 9.0.3 returns them as-is (surrogate pair unreduced).
Identifies the Tcl version in use; Tcl 9.0.3 changed surrogate handling compared to earlier versions, which is the likely root cause of the encoding mismatch.
Confirms only test_tcl failed out of 505 tests, and both failures are surrogate-related.
The failure is reproducible (not flaky): the test failed on both the initial run and the re-run in verbose mode.

suggested_fix: Update test_eval_surrogates_in_result and test_evalfile_surrogates_in_result in Lib/test/test_tcl.py to match the new surrogate handling behavior of Tcl 9.0.3, or fix the _tkinter C extension's string decoding logic to convert surrogate pairs from Tcl 9 back into the combined Unicode codepoint before returning to Python.
